verses.3: But let the righteous be glad.
verses.4: Sing to God! Sing praises to his name!
verses.5: A father of the fatherless, and a defender of the widows,
verses.6: God sets the lonely in families.
verses.7: God, when you went out before your people,
verses.9: You, God, sent a plentiful rain.
verses.13: while you sleep among the camp fires,
verses.14: When the Almighty scattered kings in her,
verses.15: The mountains of Bashan are majestic mountains.
verses.16: Why do you look in envy, you rugged mountains,
verses.17: The chariots of God are tens of thousands and thousands of thousands.
verses.19: Blessed be the Lord, who daily bears our burdens,
verses.20: God is to us a God of deliverance.
verses.21: But God will strike through the head of his enemies,
verses.22: The Lord said, “I will bring you again from Bashan,
verses.23: that you may crush them, dipping your foot in blood,
verses.24: They have seen your processions, God,
verses.25: The singers went before, the minstrels followed after,
verses.27: There is little Benjamin, their ruler,
verses.28: Your God has commanded your strength.
verses.29: Because of your temple at Jerusalem,
verses.30: Rebuke the wild animal of the reeds,
verses.31: Princes shall come out of Egypt.
verses.32: Sing to God, you kingdoms of the earth!
verses.33: to him who rides on the heaven of heavens, which are of old;
verses.35: You are awesome, God, in your sanctuaries.
